key_insight: A dual-network PINN can solve a mass-spring-damper optimal control problem while enforcing terminal state constraints exactly and matching classical optimal-control solutions closely.
engineering_relevance: This offers a reproducible, lower-barrier workflow for engineers who want to prototype optimal control with neural networks instead of deriving and solving adjoint or boundary-value formulations.
method: State network uses a composite cubic-and-mask ansatz to satisfy boundary conditions exactly
Control network remains unconstrained and is optimized jointly with the state network
Loss blends physics residuals at collocation points with a trapezoidal cost approximation using one scalar weight
evidence_signal: In the benchmark, the PINN reproduces the classical optimal cost to four significant digits and yields state/control errors within the spread of two classical reference methods.
watch_item: Training is reported to be about two orders of magnitude slower than classical shooting on this benchmark, so compute cost and tuning sensitivity matter.
paper_title: Dual-Network PINNs for Optimal Control: A Reproducible Benchmark on the Mass-Spring-Damper System
